explicit_text: I am the CEO of Classical King FM, the classical music station serving the Puget Sound region including Seattle and Bellevue. We are a nonprofit public radio station, and my role involves overseeing all operations and fundraising. During the COVID-19 pandemic, we faced unique challenges like moving into a new building just before lockdowns and deciding to keep core staff working on-site to maintain live programming. We also made programming decisions to provide our listeners a mental health break by not mentioning COVID on air and introducing interactive elements like a weekly all-request day.
implicit_text: I am the CEO of a nonprofit public radio station dedicated to classical music serving the Puget Sound region including Seattle and Bellevue. My role involves overseeing all operations and fundraising efforts. During the early months of the COVID-19 crisis, we faced unique challenges like moving into a new building just before lockdowns and deciding to keep core staff working on-site to maintain live programming. We also made programming decisions to provide our listeners a mental health break by not mentioning the pandemic on air and introducing interactive elements like a weekly all-request day.

explicit_text: Puget Sound is the region that Classical King FM serves, including cities like Seattle and Bellevue. It’s an important area for us because our listeners live here and rely on us for classical music and comfort, especially during difficult times like the COVID-19 pandemic. Being connected to this community shapes how we operate and what programming decisions we make to support our audience’s mental health and well-being.
implicit_text: This region includes cities like Seattle and Bellevue and is the primary area served by the classical music station I lead. It’s an important community for us because our listeners live here and rely on us for classical music and comfort, especially during difficult times like the COVID-19 pandemic. Being connected to this area shapes how we operate and what programming decisions we make to support our audience’s mental health and well-being.

explicit_text: Seattle is one of the key cities in the Puget Sound region that Classical King FM serves. Our station’s presence here is vital because many of our listeners are from Seattle, and they depend on us for soothing and intelligent classical music. During the pandemic, being in Seattle meant adapting to local health guidelines and supporting our community through challenging times by providing a safe space on air.

explicit_text: Bellevue, Washington is part of the Puget Sound area that Classical King FM serves alongside Seattle. Our listeners in Bellevue are important to us, and we consider their needs when making programming choices, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic. Being aware of the community’s experiences in Bellevue helps us tailor our broadcasts to offer comfort and connection through classical music.
implicit_text: This city, part of the broader region we serve alongside Seattle, is home to many of our listeners. We consider the needs of this community when making programming choices, especially during the COVID-19 crisis. Being aware of their experiences helps us tailor our broadcasts to offer comfort and connection through classical music.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic drastically changed how we operated at Classical King FM starting in early 2020. We had just moved into a new building when the stay-at-home order was issued, which made it challenging to adapt quickly. I focused heavily on keeping our staff safe by implementing strict health protocols and deciding to keep core programming staff working on-site to maintain live broadcasts. We also chose not to mention COVID on air to provide listeners a mental health break, and introduced interactive programming like a weekly all-request day to help people feel connected during isolation.
